title: Term-to-Value bridge — bidirectional serde_json::Value conversion
purpose: The Meridian workflow executor passes inputs as HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> and expects outputs as serde_json::Value. Gleam functions operate on BEAM terms. A bidirectional converter between Term and serde_json::Value is needed for the beamr-meridian integration layer. This module belongs in the beamr core crate because any host program embedding beamr will need JSON interop.
task: Implement a term::json module with term_to_value(Term) -> serde_json::Value and value_to_term(Value, &mut ProcessContext) -> Term conversions.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement custom struct serialization (no derive macros)
SHALL NOT add serde as a required dependency of beamr — gate behind a json feature flag
SHALL handle all immediate and common boxed term types

requirements.title: Term to serde_json::Value
requirements.spec: Convert a BEAM Term to a serde_json::Value: SmallInt→Number, Atom(true/false)→Bool, Atom(nil/undefined)→Null, Atom(other)→String, Nil→Array(empty), Binary(UTF-8)→String, Binary(non-UTF-8)→String(base64), Tuple→Array, Cons→Array(flatten), Map→Object, Float→Number, Pid→String(<0.N.0>), BigInt→Number or String for overflow.

requirements.title: serde_json::Value to Term
requirements.spec: Convert a serde_json::Value to a BEAM Term: Null→NIL, Bool(true)→atom(TRUE), Bool(false)→atom(FALSE), Number(int)→small_int, Number(float)→Float(heap), String→Binary(heap), Array→Cons list, Object→Map(heap). Heap allocation uses ProcessContext::alloc_tuple, alloc_cons, and leaked-heap patterns for binaries.
acceptance: value_to_term(Value::String("hello"), ctx) returns a binary term
value_to_term(Value::Array([1, 2, 3]), ctx) returns a proper cons list
value_to_term(Value::Object({"key": "value"}), ctx) returns a map term
Round-trip: term_to_value(value_to_term(v)) preserves structure for all JSON types
Tests cover each type and round-trip

requirements.title: AtomTable integration for string-atom conversion
requirements.spec: term_to_value needs the AtomTable to look up atom names. value_to_term needs it to intern atom strings. Add an AtomTable parameter (or make it available via ProcessContext).
acceptance: term_to_value(atom_term, atom_table) produces the correct string
value_to_term(Value::String("ok"), ctx) when result should be an atom — keys in objects become atoms
No panics for unknown atom IDs
